name: Cicely Tyson
abstract: Cecily Louise "Cicely" Tyson (; December 19, 1924 – January 28, 2021) was an American actress. In a career spanning seven decades, she portrayed complex and strong-willed African American women.

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I said, no, his birthday was the 26th. It was on the 26th. So when I went back on the mic, I told people how I had met Cicely Tyson. She was reading some copy for me on a play that I was fortunate enough to be the producer of.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Cicely Tyson, here recently, was a great television show about the saga of a life of Miss Jane Pittman. Was on television. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, oh, Jane Pittman. Jane Pittman.

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: one night I emceed for Miles, one of Miles’s groups... [INTERVIEWER] Cicely Tyson? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. She’s sitting over in the wings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: She's -- oh, she's fantastic. She's a superb actress, you know.
